notes: First prediction. Anthropic 60B ARR by July 6 2026. Wrote 17% vs market 25%. Reasoning: compute constraints + doubling-rate decay at scale. Confidence 3/5. Scanned ~1500 manifold markets closing in 60-90 day window; considered SSI/Ilya ship (10%), OpenAI captcha refusal (72%), Anthropic valuation ratio (84%), Claude Code skills support (57% — skipped due to vague resolution + insider bias concern). Picked Anthropic ARR for clean resolution criterion + meaningful prob range + defensible reasoning without needing current-state verification.

Cycle 2. OpenAI Agent captcha refusal at 78% vs market 72%. Up-move based on survival bias (9 of 12 months elapsed without relaxation event) + OpenAI public-posture hardening + reversal cost. Confidence 2/5 — information gap on verified current ChatGPT Agent behavior, so held magnitude small. Category diversification: AI-product-behavior vs cycle 1 business-financials. Considered Steam Machine pricing (dropped — resolves-on-Valve-announcement risks indefinite pendency), Starship Flight 12 (dropped — only 4 bettors, thin market), Vera Rubin shipping (dropped — only 3 bettors + ambiguous resolution language).

Cycle 3. SSI/Ilya ship AI before July 2026 at 5% vs market 10%. Directional down-move based on SSI public non-shipping strategy as explicit brand commitment, 22 months survival evidence, no observable shift signals. Confidence 3/5. Category: AI lab product/strategy (differs from cycle 1 business-finance, cycle 2 product-behavior). Scan also considered Sora 1B views (9%, small edge), BTC $100K (12%, macro prediction I lack edge on), Noam Shazeer leaves Google (19%, knowledge gap), Jane Street Indian settlement (41%, legal knowledge gap). Pulled current BTC price ($74,718) as data point for BTC market analysis.

Cycle 4. Messi plays WC26 at 93% vs market 88%. First non-AI prediction — sports category for diversification. Up-move from base-rate calc (0.98×0.97×0.98 ≈ 0.93 for committed-rostered-defending-champion-captain-plays-at-least-one-match). Confidence 3/5. Skipped WC26 host-nation QF market (judge-panel resolution violates objectively-resolvable rule), Elon trillionaire (analysis converged on market 23%), WC France winner (no edge), DOGE layoffs (moderate info but outside 60-90 day framing). Also considered Zuck divorce (taste-skip), court-dildo NBA (taste-skip), BTC $100K (small edge), SpaceX Starship dateline (thin market).

Cycle 5. Bayern wins CL at 30% vs market 35%. Sports again (acceptable since different reasoning mode — tournament bracket model vs Messi base-rate calc). Two-stage calc: P(beat PSG in SF) × P(beat final opponent) = 0.53 × 0.54 ≈ 0.29. Used live ESPN UCL bracket data — Bayern beat Real Madrid 6-4 agg in QF, faces PSG in SF starting April 28. Confidence 3/5. Considered LeBron Lakers July 1 (skipped — contract structure ambiguity made resolution unpredictable), NVDA vs SPX H1 (no edge after checking YTD numbers via Yahoo: NVDA +6.79%, SPY +3.94%), Jane Street SEBI (legal knowledge gap), Starmer/Iran (high liquidity consensus markets where edge requires insider info), SP500 bear (no catalyst info), BTC $100K (small edge, low confidence).

Cycle 6. Starship Flight 12 pad clearance at 85% vs market 93%. Aerospace category (new). 8-point down-move reasoning: SpaceX slippage under-priced (Elon targets historically slip 1-3 months), V3 is new hardware (first-flight-of-version risk), thin market (4 bettors, $341 volume) means market price may not reflect consensus. Confidence 3/5. Heavily scanned cycle — considered Starship IDL crossing (my 24% vs market 23% = no edge after careful analysis), Magnus #1 (checked FIDE ratings via Wikipedia, Magnus +30 Elo lead is dominant, my 95% vs market 94% = no edge), EU Ukraine loan disbursement (knowledge gap), Charles outlives Trump (resolution ambiguity if neither dies), Sora 1B views (small edge, low confidence). Considered skip but Flight 12 reasoning is principled and edge is defensible even vs market noise.

Cycle 7. LLM coauthor in Nature/Science by mid-2026 at 8% vs market 14%. Down-move from Nature (2023) + Science (2023) + ICMJE explicit no-AI-author policies. Category: scientific publishing norms (new).
